items.title: Gemini sent $10M in Bitcoin to Trump PAC after joint motion with CFTC
items.summary: The Bitcoin donation can be used to support Donald Trump as a New York court considers whether to reverse a $5 million settlement between the CFTC and Gemini.

items.title: Rust-proof your code with our new Testing Handbook chapter
items.summary: We’ve added a new chapter to our Testing Handbook: a comprehensive guide to security testing Rust programs. This chapter covers the tools and techniques we use at Trail of Bits to validate the security of Rust programs and systems. fn main() {(|f:&dyn Fn(u128)->Box< dyn Iterator<

items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Goldman Sachs CEO backs Clarity Act despite banking industry's concerns over stablecoin rules
items.summary: The Goldman Sachs CEO said the crypto market structure bill would create a more stable regulatory framework, breaking with other major bank leaders who oppose key stablecoin provisions.

items.title: Polymarket to challenge France’s nationwide website block
items.summary: France’s regulator has said the platform still promoted illegal gambling and cited loss, identity-check and market-manipulation risks.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin slides as stocks slump on Iran war expansion, AI spending concern
items.summary: Chipmaker Intel's revenue beat helped ease sentiment after a bruising session for tech stocks, with bitcoin holding near $65,000.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Live updates: Dogecoin and ether lead pullback as investors digest tech earnings
items.summary: Dogecoin dropped 4.5% and ether 2.5%, leading a broad but shallow retreat across the majors, though most remain higher on the week ahead of next week's Fed meeting.

items.title: Ripple’s RLUSD gets two boosts as transfer volume drops 25%
items.summary: Ripple launched an institutional minting platform and added RLUSD to Notabene’s compliance network as holder growth rose but monthly transfer volume fell to $10.95 billion.

items.title: Mirae plans to turn crypto exchange Korbit into something Korea hasn't seen before
items.summary: The South Korean financial giant says its Korbit – soon to be rebranded as Digital X – is just the starting point for a broader ecosystem that connects RWAs, stablecoins and digital assets.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $65,000 as $800 billion AI selloff leaves crypto largely untouched
items.summary: The Magnificent Seven had their worst day since April 2025 after Alphabet and Tesla spooked investors on AI spending. Bitcoin fell less than 1%, and dogecoin led the majors lower.

items.title: South Korea’s Korbit to rebrand as Digital X under Mirae: Report
items.summary: Mirae Asset reportedly plans to use the South Korean crypto exchange as a hub for tokenized assets, stablecoins and digital finance.

items.title: Ripple launches Mint to expand institutional access to RLUSD
items.summary: Ripple launches Mint to expand RLUSD access for institutions as the stablecoin’s market cap approaches $1.6 billion.

items.title: Bitcoin mining pool Poolin files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
items.summary: Bitcoin miner Poolin filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and initiated a $52 million sale of its two West Texas mining sites as part of its future creditor recovery program.

items.title: EU widens Belarus ownership ban to all crypto service providers
items.summary: The EU will bar Belarusian nationals and residents from owning, controlling or managing exchanges and other MiCA-regulated crypto firms from Aug. 25.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs snap seven-day inflow streak with $225M in outflows
items.summary: After attracting nearly $1 billion over seven trading sessions, US-listed spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded their first daily net outflow since July 13.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: BitMEX hit with 623 BTC lawsuit on day it announces shutdown
items.summary: The proposed class action alleges BitMEX used privileged trading access and server freezes to profit from forced liquidations.

items.title: Dem senator calls GOP’s CLARITY ethics proposal a ‘piece of s---’: Politico
items.summary: On Wednesday, Senate Republicans released the proposed text for the CLARITY Act, which has been met with pushback from Democrats regarding ethics provisions.

items.title: BitMEX delists 65 trading pairs, derivatives in July amid exchange shutdown
items.summary: BitMEX will have removed 65 derivative contracts and trading pairs in July, compared with just 19 across the first six months of the year.
